basic_details.job_title: AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Recruitment 2026 - Walk in for 70 Posts
basic_details.short_information: All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), Bilaspur, Himachal Pradesh, has released a notification for a walk-in interview to fill 70 Senior Resident (Non-Academic) posts across Broad Specialty and Super Specialty departments. The notification, numbered AIIMS-BLS(B)(2)(04)26-766 and dated 15.07.2026, states that candidates are not required to fill any online application form. Instead, eligible candidates must report in person at the Recruitment Cell, 3rd Floor, Administrative Block, AIIMS Bilaspur, Kothipura, Himachal Pradesh-174037, on or before 09:00 AM on 21 July 2026.

important_dates.raw: Notification Release Date: 15.07.2026 Walk-in Interview / Reporting Date: 21.07.2026 (Tuesday) Reporting Time: On or before 09:00 AM Last Entry for Candidates: Candidates reporting after 10:00 AM will not be allowed | The notification does not mention any separate "last date to apply" since this is a walk-in interview and no online application form needs to be submitted in advance.

application_fee.details: PwBD/SC/ST candidates: Exempted from payment of fee All other categories: Rs. 1,000 + 18% GST = Rs. 1,180 The application fee must be paid through NEFT to the bank account mentioned below. The fee is non-refundable. Candidates must upload proof of payment and also produce it at the time of the interview.

age_limit.details: Upper Age Limit: 45 years (age as on crucial date) Upper age limit shall be determined as on the last date of submission of applications. Date of Birth as recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary Examination Certificate only will be accepted for determining age; no subsequent request for change will be considered or granted. No age relaxation is available to reserved category candidates applying for unreserved vacancies. Permissible relaxation of the upper age limit as per Government orders shall be allowed, subject to submission of documentary proof of the category under which age relaxation is sought.

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: Post Graduate Medical Degree - MD/DNB in General Medicine; DM/DrNB in Cardiology/Medical Gastroenterology/Medical Oncology/Nephrology from a recognized University/Institute
Post Graduate Medical Degree - MS/DNB in General Surgery; MCh/DrNB in Burns & Plastic Surgery from a recognised University/Institute

Post Graduate Medical Degree - MS/DNB in General Surgery; MCh/DrNB in CTVS; MCh/DrNB in Urology; MCh/DrNB in Paediatrics Surgery, Surgical Gastroenterology, Surgical Oncology from a recognised University/Institute
Post Graduate Medical Degree - MD/DNB in Paediatrics; or DM/DrNB in Neonatology from a recognised University/Institute
Post Graduate Medical Degree - MD/DNB in Medicine, MS in General Surgery & Allied and Orthopaedics from a recognised University/Institute
Post Graduate Medical Degree - MD/DNB in Chest and TB or MD/DNB in Medicine; DM/DrNB in Pulmonary Medicine from a recognised University/Institute
Post Graduate Medical Degree - MD/DNB in General Medicine; DM/DrNB in Endocrinology from a recognised University/Institute

Medical: A postgraduate medical degree, i.e., MD/DNB in Anatomy from a recognized university/institute, or equivalent qualification. Non-Medical: M.Sc./M.Biotech degree in the subject concerned AND Ph.D. in the subject concerned/allied subject from a recognized University/Institute.

Medical: A postgraduate medical degree, i.e., MD/DNB in Biochemistry from a recognized university/institute, or equivalent qualification. Non-Medical: M.Sc./M.Biotech degree in the subject concerned AND Ph.D. in the subject concerned/allied subject from a recognized University/Institute.

Medical: A postgraduate medical degree, i.e., MD/DNB in Pharmacology OR DM (Clinical Pharmacology) from a recognized university/institute, or equivalent qualification. Non-Medical: M.Sc./M.Biotech degree in the subject concerned AND Ph.D. in the subject concerned/allied subject from a recognized University/Institute.

Medical: Post Graduate Medical Degree - MD/DNB in General Medicine; DM/DrNB in Clinical Immunology/Rheumatology from a recognized University/Institute. Non-Medical: M.Sc./M.Biotech degree in the subject concerned AND Ph.D. in the subject concerned/allied subject (preferably in the area of basic and translational immunology or rheumatology) from a recognized University/Institute.

qualification.details: A medical qualification included in the first or second schedule, or Part II of the third schedule, of the Indian Medical Council Act 1956 (persons possessing qualifications included in Part II of the third schedule must also fulfill the conditions specified in Section 13(3) of the Act). Must be registered with the Central/State Medical Council. A postgraduate degree, i.e., MD/MS/DNB as per MCI rules in the specialty concerned, or its equivalent from a recognized University/Institute. The candidate applying for these posts should have a valid qualification (pass certificate) as on the last date for receipt of applications. Candidates possessing relevant MD/MS/DNB qualification may also apply for certain super specialty departments as per the specific eligibility table below.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Notification Release Date: 15.07.2026 Walk-in Interview / Reporting Date: 21.07.2026 (Tuesday) Reporting Time: On or before 09:00 AM Last Entry for Candidates: Candidates reporting after 10:00 AM will not be allowed The notification does not mention any separate "last date to apply" since this is a walk-in interview and no online application form needs to be submitted in advance.

content_sections.heading: AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: The recruitment covers two categories of Senior Resident (Non-Academic) posts: Broad Specialty and Super Specialty. The category-wise vacancy breakup as given in the official notification is below. 4% of total posts are reserved for the PwBD category (non-surgical jobs) among Senior Resident posts. Abbreviations (Category): UR = Un-reserved, SC = Scheduled Caste, ST = Scheduled Tribe, OBC = Other Backward Classes, EWS = Economically Weaker Section, PwBD = Persons with Benchmark Disabilities. Abbreviations (Physical Requirement): S = Sitting, ST = Standing, W = Walking, RW = Reading & Writing, SE = Seeing, H = Hearing, BN = Bending, L = Lifting, KC = Kneeling & Crouching, PP = Pulling & Pushing, MF = Manipulation with Fingers, C = Communication. Abbreviations (Disability): OA = One Arm, OL = One Leg, BL = Both Leg, OAL = One Arm and One Leg, CP = Cerebral Palsy, LC = Leprosy Cured, Dw = Dwarfism, AAV = Acid Attack Victims, SLD = Specific Learning Disability, MD = Multiple Disabilities. Unreserved category candidates can also apply against reserved posts, but they will be considered only if a reserved category candidate is not available. In such cases, their engagement shall be for a period of 44 days only, from the date of joining.

tables.caption: Reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD)

rows.physical_requirements: S, ST, W, RW, SE, H
rows.categories_of_disabilities: a) OA, OL, BL, OAL, CP, LC, Dw, AAV (Non-surgical jobs) b) SLD c) MD involving (a) to (b) above

content_sections.heading: AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: A medical qualification included in the first or second schedule, or Part II of the third schedule, of the Indian Medical Council Act 1956 (persons possessing qualifications included in Part II of the third schedule must also fulfill the conditions specified in Section 13(3) of the Act). Must be registered with the Central/State Medical Council. A postgraduate degree, i.e., MD/MS/DNB as per MCI rules in the specialty concerned, or its equivalent from a recognized University/Institute. The candidate applying for these posts should have a valid qualification (pass certificate) as on the last date for receipt of applications. Candidates possessing relevant MD/MS/DNB qualification may also apply for certain super specialty departments as per the specific eligibility table below.

content_sections.heading: AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Age Limit
content_sections.text: Upper Age Limit: 45 years (age as on crucial date) Upper age limit shall be determined as on the last date of submission of applications. Date of Birth as recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary Examination Certificate only will be accepted for determining age; no subsequent request for change will be considered or granted. No age relaxation is available to reserved category candidates applying for unreserved vacancies. Permissible relaxation of the upper age limit as per Government orders shall be allowed, subject to submission of documentary proof of the category under which age relaxation is sought.

content_sections.heading: AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Salary and Pay Scale

rows.additional_allowances: Admissible allowances as applicable to Govt. of India employees stationed at Bilaspur, H.P.
Admissible allowances as applicable to Govt. of India employees stationed at Bilaspur, H.P. (NPA not applicable, as it is only for medical graduates)

content_sections.heading: Tenure of the Post
content_sections.text: The tenure of the post is three years, provided the candidate's overall past residency period at AIIMS Bilaspur or any other institute does not exceed three years. Candidates must declare their previous senior residency details in the application form under "Professional Experience" (Point No. 18).

content_sections.heading: AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Application Fee
content_sections.text: PwBD/SC/ST candidates: Exempted from payment of fee All other categories: Rs. 1,000 + 18% GST = Rs. 1,180 The application fee must be paid through NEFT to the bank account mentioned below. The fee is non-refundable. Candidates must upload proof of payment and also produce it at the time of the interview.

content_sections.heading: Reservation Certificate Requirements
content_sections.text: OBC Candidates: Must attach a certificate issued by the competent authority, valid for posts under the Central Government of India, clearly stating that the candidate does not belong to the Creamy Layer. Since the vacancies are advertised for financial year 2026-27, only OBC-NCL certificates issued on or after 01.04.2026 will be considered valid. EWS Candidates: Persons not covered under SC/ST/OBC reservation, whose family has a gross annual income below Rs. 8 lakh, are identified as EWS. Income includes salary, agriculture, business, profession, etc., for the financial year prior to the year of application. A valid income certificate in the prescribed format is required at the time of application and must be submitted at document verification. An EWS certificate issued on or after 01.04.2026, based on income for financial year 2026-2027, will be considered valid for the year 2026-2027. SC, ST, OBC & EWS: Certificates must be issued by a Tehsildar or above rank authority, as prescribed by the Government of India, in the prescribed Central Government format.

content_sections.heading: AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is through a Walk-in Interview, held at the Administrative Block, 3rd Floor, AIIMS Bilaspur, Kothipura, Himachal Pradesh-174037. Candidates are not required to fill any online application form. Candidates must appear physically before the interview board; no request for an online interview will be entertained. Based on verification of original documents, the Screening Committee may shortlist candidates for the interview. The decision of the Competent Authority, AIIMS Bilaspur, in this regard shall be final and binding. The offer of appointment, when made, will be provisional and subject to verification of credentials by the competent authority. A wait list may be maintained. Any vacancy arising due to non-joining or resignation of selected candidates will be offered to candidates from the waiting list, according to merit.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Interview
content_sections.text: The duly filled and signed original application form must be produced at the time of interview, along with one (1) set of self-attested photocopies of the following documents. Originals must be produced for verification at the time of the interview: Degrees and Certificates Mark sheets Age proof Caste certificates (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS, as applicable) PwBD certificate (if applicable) Publications (Pub-Med, Non-PubMed), Awards, etc. (if any) Annexure-II: Proforma/Checklist for the post of Senior Resident (filled and submitted during document verification) MBBS degree certificate, Internship Completion Certificate Qualifying degree certificate (MBBS/MD/MS/MDS/DNB/DM/M.Ch./M.Sc./Ph.D., as applicable) Medical registration certificate Date of Birth Certificate / 10th Standard Certificate No Objection Certificate (NOC), for candidates working in Central/State Government/Semi-Government/Autonomous Institutions, clearly stating that the candidate will be permitted to join AIIMS Bilaspur in the event of selection. Candidates will be allowed to appear for the interview only on production of the original NOC. Incomplete forms or applications without the required documents will not be considered for further processing.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: There is no online application form for this recruitment. Candidates must report in person. Report at the Recruitment Cell, 3rd Floor, Administrative Block, AIIMS Bilaspur, Kothipura, Himachal Pradesh, on or before 09:00 AM on 21.07.2026. Carry the duly filled and signed original application form, along with one set of self-attested photocopies of all required documents and originals for verification. Pay the applicable application fee via NEFT in advance and carry proof of payment to the interview. Submit the Annexure-II Proforma/Checklist during document verification, which takes place before the interview. Appear physically before the interview board. Candidates reporting after 10:00 AM will not be allowed.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is no separate last date to apply, since this is a walk-in interview and no online application form is required. Candidates must report in person on or before 09:00 AM on 21.07.2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 70 total vacancies: 43 posts under Broad Specialty and 27 posts under Super Specialty departments.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident posts?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 45 years, as on the crucial date, calculated based on the Date of Birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary Examination Certificate.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary for AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident (Non-Academic)?
faq.answer: Medical candidates (MD/MS/MCh/DM/DNB) are placed in Level-11 of the pay matrix with a minimum of Rs. 67,700/- plus NPA and admissible allowances. Non-medical candidates (M.Sc. with Ph.D.) are placed in Level-10 with a minimum of Rs. 56,100/- plus admissible allowances (NPA not applicable).

faq.question: Q5. Is there an application fee for AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates other than PwBD/SC/ST must pay Rs. 1,000 plus 18% GST, totaling Rs. 1,180, via NEFT to the specified bank account. PwBD, SC, and ST candidates are exempted from the fee.

faq.question: Q6. Is AIIMS Bilaspur Senior Resident Recruitment 2026 through online application or walk-in interview?
faq.answer: It is entirely a walk-in interview process. Candidates are not required to fill any online application form and must report in person at the Recruitment Cell with the required documents.
